title: Robust Hearty Brazilian Chicken Stew
description: A grassy, shattering, hangover-cure Brazilian dish featuring dry sherry, clay-pot-cook, whole30-friendly ingredients, this stew is perfect for a cold winter night, it is a hearty and flavorful meal that will leave you feeling satisfied and warm.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the chicken thighs with salt and black pepper.
Heat the coconut oil in a large clay pot over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped bell pepper to the pot and cook until it is tender.
Add the chopped tomato to the pot and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the chicken thighs to the pot and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the dry sherry and chicken broth to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Cook the stew for 30 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
